hawkish_phrases: inflation continued to run well above 2 percent and generally judged the risks to the outlook for inflation as tilted to the upside
most participants suggested that a faster pace of increases in the target range for the federal funds rate than in the post-2015 period would likely be warranted
labor market conditions as already at or very close to those consistent with maximum employment
if inflation does not move down as they expect, it would be appropriate for the Committee to remove policy accommodation at a faster pace than they currently anticipate
conditions would likely warrant beginning to reduce the size of the balance sheet sometime later this year
dovish_phrases: keep the target range for the federal funds rate at 0 to 1/4 percent
Progress on vaccinations and an easing of supply constraints were expected to support continued gains in economic activity
the Omicron wave of the pandemic would weigh on economic activity in the first quarter of 2022
longer-term measures of inflation expectations appeared to remain well anchored
maintaining flexibility to implement appropriate policy adjustments on the basis of risk-management considerations
summary: The Committee unanimously held rates at 0-0.25% but signaled that rate increases would soon be appropriate and balance sheet reduction would begin later in 2022, with most participants favoring a faster pace of tightening than in 2015 given elevated inflation well above 2% and a labor market viewed as at or near maximum employment.
